docs.title: MemoryBase
docs.text:
The project operating system for AI agents.

MemoryBase gives your AI coding assistants a shared memory and task system. Agents can read your project blueprint, update task statuses, record decisions, and coordinate with each other — all through a standardised MCP interface.

docs.title: Why MemoryBase?
docs.text:
Modern AI coding workflows break down as soon as you have more than one agent, more than one session, or more than a few days of work. Context gets lost, tasks get duplicated, decisions go undocumented.

MemoryBase solves this with three primitives:

Primitive What it does Blueprint Structured project definition — tech stack, folder layout, constraints, API specs Tasks Kanban-style task tracker with phase/milestone grouping and real-time SSE updates Vault Searchable log of decisions, error patterns, and architectural notes

All three are accessible to any AI agent via MCP tools, and to humans via a React web UI.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 17 MCP tools covering projects, tasks, and vault — works with Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client
  • Real-time updates via Server-Sent Events — the UI reflects agent changes instantly
  • Kanban board with drag-and-drop, phase filtering, and SSE live sync
  • Blueprint editor with in-place editing and structured JSON views
  • Vault explorer with full-text search and tag filtering
  • Zero config defaults — SQLite out of the box, PostgreSQL for production
  • Single Docker image — ships frontend and backend together

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip (fastest)DockerLocal dev 
pip install memorybase
memorybase --port 8000

Open http://localhost:8000 — done.

docker run -p 8000:8000 \
  -e MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=my-secret \
  ghcr.io/sabbiramin113008/memorybase:latest

git clone https://github.com/sabbiramin113008/memorybase.git
cd memorybase
./start-backend.sh   # terminal 1
./start-frontend.sh  # terminal 2

For full setup instructions, see Getting Started.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
MemoryBase exposes a REST API at /api/*. Interactive documentation (Swagger UI) is available at:

http://localhost:8000/docs

ReDoc alternative:

http://localhost:8000/redoc

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All /api/* endpoints require the X-MemoryBase-Key header:

curl http://localhost:8000/api/projects \
  -H "X-MemoryBase-Key: your-api-key"

Returns 401 if the header is missing or incorrect.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /api/projects List all projects POST /api/projects Create a project GET /api/projects/{id} Get a project PATCH /api/projects/{id} Update a project DELETE /api/projects/{id} Delete a project GET /api/projects/{id}/blueprint Get blueprint PATCH /api/projects/{id}/blueprint Update blueprint GET /api/projects/{id}/skills List skills POST /api/projects/{id}/skills Create skill PATCH /api/projects/{id}/skills/{skill_id} Update skill

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /api/projects/{id}/tasks List tasks (filterable by status, phase) POST /api/projects/{id}/tasks Create task GET /api/projects/{id}/tasks/{task_id} Get task PATCH /api/projects/{id}/tasks/{task_id} Update task PATCH /api/projects/{id}/tasks/{task_id}/status Update task status + append note DELETE /api/projects/{id}/tasks/{task_id} Delete task

docs.title: Vault
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /api/projects/{id}/vault List entries (filterable by type, tags) POST /api/projects/{id}/vault Create entry GET /api/projects/{id}/vault/{entry_id} Get entry GET /api/projects/{id}/vault/search?q= Full-text search DELETE /api/projects/{id}/vault/{entry_id} Delete entry

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /api/settings Get current LLM settings (no secrets returned) PATCH /api/settings Update LLM provider / model / API key

docs.title: Real-time Events
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /events/{project_id} SSE stream for a project

docs.title: Meta
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /health Health check — returns {"status": "ok", "version": "0.1.0"}

docs.title: Project
docs.text:
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "name": "My Project",
  "description": "...",
  "domain": "developer-tools",
  "status": "active",
  "created_at": "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Task
docs.text:
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "title": "Implement auth",
  "description": "...",
  "phase": "Phase 1",
  "milestone": "MVP",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "acceptance_criteria": "...",
  "agent_notes": "[2026-04-22] Started.\n",
  "blocked_reason": null,
  "created_at": "...",
  "updated_at": "..."
}

docs.title: Vault Entry
docs.text:
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "entry_type": "decision",
  "summary": "Use SQLite for development",
  "detail": "Chosen for zero-config local dev. PostgreSQL for prod.",
  "tags": ["database", "architecture"],
  "created_at": "..."
}

docs.title: SSE Event
docs.text:
{
  "event": "task.updated",
  "project_id": "uuid",
  "data": { "task_id": "uuid", "new_status": "done" },
  "timestamp": "2026-04-22T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: MemoryBase is configured entirely through environment variables. All variables can also be placed in a .env file in the project root — pydantic-settings reads it automatically.

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Type Default Description DATABASE_URL str sqlite:///./memorybase.db SQLAlchemy database URL. Use postgresql://user:pass@host/db for production. MEMORYBASE_API_KEY str dev-key-change-in-production Secret key sent in X-MemoryBase-Key header. Must be changed in production. HOST str 0.0.0.0 Host to bind the uvicorn server to. PORT int 8000 Port to listen on. CORS_ORIGINS list[str] ["http://localhost:5173", "http://localhost:3000"] Allowed CORS origins. STATIC_DIR str ../frontend/dist Path to the frontend build output. Served at / when the directory exists. MEMORYBASE_LLM_PROVIDER str anthropic Default LLM provider (for future LLM features). MEMORYBASE_LLM_MODEL str claude-sonnet-4-20250514 Default LLM model. MEMORYBASE_LLM_API_KEY str "" API key for the LLM provider.

docs.title: Example .env File
docs.text:
# Database
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./memorybase.db

# Security — change this before deploying!
MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=my-strong-secret-key

# Network
HOST=0.0.0.0
PORT=8000

# CORS — add your frontend URL
CORS_ORIGINS=["http://localhost:5273", "https://yourdomain.com"]

# LLM (optional)
MEMORYBASE_LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic
MEMORYBASE_LLM_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
MEMORYBASE_LLM_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

docs.title: PostgreSQL Setup
docs.text:
To switch from SQLite to PostgreSQL, install the driver and set the URL:

pip install psycopg2-binary

DATABASE_URL=postgresql://memorybase:password@localhost:5432/memorybase

MemoryBase uses SQLModel (SQLAlchemy under the hood) and works with any SQLAlchemy-compatible database.

docs.title: Runtime Settings
docs.text:
LLM provider and model can also be changed at runtime via PATCH /api/settings. These settings are persisted to the database and take precedence over environment variables after the first save.

Field Description llm_provider Provider name (e.g. anthropic, openai) llm_model Model identifier llm_api_key API key — accepted but never returned in API responses

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to MemoryBase!

docs.title: 1. Fork and clone
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/sabbiramin113008/memorybase.git
cd memorybase

docs.title: 2. Backend
docs.text:
# Create venv
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate

# Install deps
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt

# Start with reload
uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000

docs.title: 3. Frontend
docs.text:
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev  # starts at http://localhost:5273

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
memorybase/
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py              # FastAPI app entry point
│   ├── config.py            # pydantic-settings configuration
│   ├── database.py          # SQLModel engine + session dependency
│   ├── auth.py              # API key verification dependency
│   ├── models/              # SQLModel table definitions + Pydantic schemas
│   │   ├── project.py       # Project, Blueprint, Skill
│   │   ├── task.py          # Task
│   │   ├── vault.py         # VaultEntry
│   │   └── settings.py      # AppSettings (persisted runtime config)
│   ├── routers/             # FastAPI routers (one per resource)
│   │   ├── projects.py
│   │   ├── tasks.py
│   │   ├── vault.py
│   │   ├── events.py        # SSE endpoint
│   │   └── settings.py
│   ├── services/
│   │   └── event_bus.py     # In-process pub/sub for SSE
│   └── mcp/
│       ├── server.py        # FastMCP instance
│       └── tools.py         # All 17 MCP tool definitions
├── frontend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── App.tsx          # Root component, router, layout
│   │   ├── lib/
│   │   │   ├── api.ts       # Typed API client
│   │   │   └── utils.ts     # cn() helper
│   │   ├── hooks/
│   │   │   └── useSSE.ts    # EventSource hook with reconnect
│   │   ├── components/ui/   # shadcn/ui components
│   │   └── pages/           # Route-level page components
│   └── vite.config.ts
├── docs/
│   └── docs/                # MkDocs source pages
├── mkdocs.yml
├── start-backend.sh
└── start-frontend.sh

docs.title: Adding a New MCP Tool
docs.text:
1. Open backend/mcp/tools.py
  2. Define a new function decorated with @mcp.tool()
  3. The function must return a JSON string (str) — use json.dumps()
  4. Call _emit(project_id, "event.type", {...}) if the tool mutates data
  5. Add an entry to docs/docs/mcp-tools.md

Example:

@mcp.tool()
def my_new_tool(project_id: str, value: str) -> str:
    """Description shown to the agent."""
    with Session(engine) as session:
        # ... do work ...
        _emit(project_id, "my.event", {"value": value})
        return json.dumps({"ok": True})

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Backend: PEP 8. Type hints everywhere. No bare except.
  • Frontend: TypeScript strict mode. No any. Prefer named exports.
  • Commits: Conventional commits (feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:).

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Backend
source venv/bin/activate
pytest

# Frontend (type check + build)
cd frontend && npm run build

docs.title: Submitting a Pull Request
docs.text:
1. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feat/my-feature
  2. Make your changes
  3. Ensure npm run build and pytest pass
  4. Open a PR against main with a clear description of the change

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • Node.js 18+
  • Git

docs.title: Option A — pip (simplest)
docs.text:
pip install memorybase
memorybase --port 8000

Open http://localhost:8000. SQLite database is created in the current directory automatically.

Set a secure API key before exposing to a network:

MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=my-secret memorybase --port 8000

docs.title: Option B — Docker
docs.text:
# 1. Pull the image
docker pull ghcr.io/sabbiramin113008/memorybase:latest

# 2. Create a data directory for SQLite persistence
mkdir -p memorybase_data

# 3. Run
docker run -p 8000:8000 \
  -v $(pwd)/memorybase_data:/app/data \
  -e MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=change-me \
  ghcr.io/sabbiramin113008/memorybase:latest

Open http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: 1. Clone the repo
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/sabbiramin113008/memorybase.git
cd memorybase

docs.title: 2. Start the backend
docs.text:
./start-backend.sh

This script:

  • Creates and activates a Python virtual environment at ./venv
  • Installs all dependencies from backend/requirements.txt
  • Starts the FastAPI server with --reload on http://localhost:8000

docs.title: 3. Start the frontend
docs.text:
In a second terminal:

./start-frontend.sh

This script:

  • Installs npm dependencies if node_modules is missing
  • Starts the Vite dev server on http://localhost:5273

The frontend proxies all /api, /events, and /mcp requests to the backend automatically.

docs.title: 1. Open the UI
docs.text: Navigate to http://localhost:5273 (dev) or http://localhost:8000 (Docker).

docs.title: 2. Create a project
docs.text: Click New Project, fill in a name, domain, and description, then click Create Project.

docs.title: 3. Define a blueprint
docs.text: Click Blueprint in the sidebar. Hover over any section and click Edit to fill in your tech stack, folder structure, and constraints.

docs.title: 4. Add tasks
docs.text: Click Kanban Board. Use the Add Task button (or the + link at the bottom of any column) to create tasks. Drag cards between columns to update status.

docs.title: 5. Connect an AI agent
docs.text:
Go to Settings and copy the MCP config for your tool:

Claude CodeCursor 
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "memorybase": {
      "type": "sse",
      "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp/sse"
    }
  }
}

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "memorybase": {
      "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp/sse"
    }
  }
}

Once connected, your agent can call tools like list_projects(), get_blueprint(), update_task_status(), and more.

See MCP Tools for the full reference.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
See Configuration for all available settings.

The most important ones to set before going to production:

MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=your-secret-key-here
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/memorybase

docs.title: MCP Tools Reference
docs.text: MemoryBase exposes all project operations as MCP tools over SSE transport. Any MCP-compliant client (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf) can connect and use these tools.

docs.title: Connection Details
docs.text:
• MCP SSE Endpoint: http://localhost:8000/mcp/sse
  • Auth: API key sent as X-MemoryBase-Key header in MCP config (not required on the SSE endpoint itself)

docs.title: Claude Code (~/.claude/mcp.json)
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "memorybase": {
      "type": "sse",
      "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp/sse",
      "headers": {
        "X-MemoryBase-Key": "your-api-key-here"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Cursor (MCP Settings)
docs.text:
{
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "memorybase": {
        "transport": "sse",
        "url": "http://localhost:8000/mcp/sse"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: list_projects()
docs.text: List all projects. Returns id, name, description, domain, status for each.

docs.title: get_project(project_id)
docs.text: Get a project by UUID. Returns full project fields.

docs.title: create_project(name, description?, domain?)
docs.text: Create a new project. Auto-creates an empty blueprint. Returns the new project.

docs.title: get_blueprint(project_id)
docs.text: Get all blueprint sections: overview, tech_stack, folder_structure, constraints, api_specs.

docs.title: update_blueprint(project_id, section, content)
docs.text:
Update one blueprint section. section must be one of: overview | tech_stack | folder_structure | constraints | api_specs

For tech_stack, pass a valid JSON string.

docs.title: get_skills(project_id)
docs.text: Get all skill profiles (backend, frontend, infra, testing).

docs.title: update_skill(project_id, skill_type, framework?, version?, libraries?, practices?)
docs.text: Upsert a skill profile. skill_type: backend | frontend | infra | testing. libraries is a JSON array string.

docs.title: list_tasks(project_id, status?, phase?)
docs.text: List tasks, optionally filtered. status: todo | in_progress | blocked | done | tested.

docs.title: get_task(project_id, task_id)
docs.text: Get a single task including agent_notes and blocked_reason.

docs.title: create_task(project_id, title, phase?, description?, acceptance_criteria?)
docs.text: Create a new task with status=todo.

docs.title: update_task_status(project_id, task_id, status, notes?, blocked_reason?)
docs.text: Update task status. Notes are appended (never overwritten) with ISO timestamp prefix. status: todo | in_progress | blocked | done | tested

docs.title: add_task_note(project_id, task_id, note)
docs.text: Append a timestamped note to agent_notes. Never overwrites existing notes.

docs.title: log_decision(project_id, summary, detail?, tags?)
docs.text: Log a decision. tags is comma-separated: "auth,database".

docs.title: log_error_pattern(project_id, summary, detail?, tags?)
docs.text: Log a known error pattern to prevent future agents from repeating it.

docs.title: log_note(project_id, entry_type, summary, detail?, tags?)
docs.text: General vault entry. entry_type: decision | error_pattern | architectural_note | agent_observation.

docs.title: get_vault(project_id, entry_type?, limit?)
docs.text: Get recent vault entries. limit defaults to 20, max 100.

docs.title: search_vault(project_id, query)
docs.text: Full-text search across summary, detail, and tags. Returns newest first.

docs.title: Typical Agent Session Flow
docs.text:
1. list_projects()                          → find your project_id
2. get_blueprint(project_id)                → load project context
3. get_vault(project_id)                    → load decisions + error patterns
4. list_tasks(project_id, status="todo")    → find next task
5. update_task_status(project_id, task_id, "in_progress", notes="Starting...")
6. ... do work ...
7. update_task_status(project_id, task_id, "done", notes="Completed X, Y, Z")
8. log_decision(project_id, summary, detail, tags)

docs.title: Docker Compose (recommended)
docs.text: The easiest way to run MemoryBase in production is Docker Compose.

docs.title: 1. Create a directory
docs.text: mkdir memorybase && cd memorybase

docs.title: 2. Create docker-compose.yml
docs.text:
services:
  memorybase:
    image: ghcr.io/sabbiramin113008/memorybase:latest
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - ./data:/app/data
    env_file: .env
    restart: unless-stopped

docs.title: 3. Create .env
docs.text:
MEMORYBASE_API_KEY=your-strong-secret-key
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:////app/data/memorybase.db

docs.title: 4. Start
docs.text: docker compose up -d

docs.title: PostgreSQL Setup
docs.text:
For production, use PostgreSQL instead of SQLite:

services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: memorybase
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
      POSTGRES_DB: memorybase
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    restart: unless-stopped

  memorybase:
    image: ghcr.io/sabbiramin113008/memorybase:latest
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://memorybase:secret@db:5432/memorybase
      MEMORYBASE_API_KEY: your-strong-secret-key
    depends_on:
      - db
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  pgdata:

docs.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy
docs.text:
To run MemoryBase behind Nginx with HTTPS:

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name memorybase.yourdomain.com;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name memorybase.yourdomain.com;

    ssl_certificate     /etc/letsencrypt/live/memorybase.yourdomain.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/memorybase.yourdomain.com/privkey.pem;

    # SSE requires buffering to be off
    proxy_buffering off;
    proxy_cache off;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:8000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;

        # Required for SSE
        proxy_read_timeout 86400s;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Connection "";
    }
}

SSE and buffering

Server-Sent Events require proxy_buffering off. Without this, real-time updates will not reach clients behind Nginx.

docs.title: Updating
docs.text:
# Pull the latest image
docker compose pull

# Restart with zero downtime
docker compose up -d --no-deps memorybase

Database migrations are handled automatically on startup — no manual steps required.
